key_departures.impact: Released after one season. Led team with 85 receptions and 1,013 receiving yards in 2025 despite cap hit of $26M. Departure opens a significant WR1 hole and creates dead money of $9.7M against the cap.
Team's leading pass rusher in 2025 with 73 pressures and 10.5 sacks — first on the team. Signed with Commanders on 1yr/$12M. A significant loss at the top of the EDGE depth chart.
Signed with Chiefs on 3yr/$21M ($14M guaranteed). Was one of the stoutest run-stuffing DTs on the roster and also showed interior pass-rush ability. Loss weakens IDL depth and run defense.
Traded to Chicago Bears for a 2027 fifth-round pick. Started all 21 games in 2025. Departure creates a clear hole at center, though Jared Wilson is expected to slide from LG to C.
Signed with Falcons on 1yr/$3.25M. Was the TE2 and a dependable blocker. His departure leaves a gap at the backup TE role behind Hunter Henry.
Signed 2yr deal with Ravens. Was a starting safety in New England's Super Bowl run. Departure triggers the Kevin Byard signing and opens a starting spot next to Craig Woodson.
Signed with 49ers on 2yr/$12M. Was the primary swing tackle and filled in capably when Will Campbell was injured in Week 12. Departure leaves the team without a proven backup OT.
Released. Was a stout edge-setter and run defender, and was the team's longest-tenured player. 25 pressures in 2025 but limited as a pass rusher. Primarily a special teams and run-defense asset.
Released. Was a rotational LB and special teams contributor who appeared in 12 games in 2025. Depth loss but not a critical starter hole.
Not tendered as an RFA. Signed with Dolphins on 1yr deal. Was a depth CB who did not start regularly. Minimal need impact.
Released after tearing his ACL in 2025 season. Was the team's primary kick returner. Depth/ST loss at RB.

contract.notes: Base deal per Albert Breer/Spotrac is 4yr/$68M with max value up to $80M per ESPN/Schefter. Guarantees not yet fully disclosed publicly; estimated significant given 4yr structure.

signings.analysis: Four-year commitment at $17M AAV (max $20M) signals real investment — Doubs is the clear WR1 replacement for Diggs. He had 55 catches, 724 yards, 6 TDs in 2025 and has posted 600+ yards in each of his last three seasons. The $17-20M AAV is solidly mid-tier at WR. However, he has never eclipsed 750 yards in a season and was never WR1 in Green Bay's crowded room. The four-year structure commits him as the starter, dropping WR from a critical tier-1 hole to a tier-2 need — the team likely still wants a true boundary WR1 upgrade (A.J. Brown trade pursuit still active) or a high-upside WR in the draft.

contract.notes: Per Spotrac/OTC: 3yr/$42M, $21M guaranteed, $7.2M signing bonus. Max value $48M including per-game roster bonuses ($250K/game, up to $12.75M total). All guaranteed money in first two years.

signings.analysis: Vera-Tucker projects as the starting LG after Jared Wilson slides from LG to C to replace traded Garrett Bradbury. When healthy (2024), he was PFF's 10th-best guard with the fourth-lowest pressure rate allowed. However, he's missed three of five seasons to injury (torn triceps, Achilles, torn triceps again), sat out all of 2025, and has played just 43 of 85 possible games. The contract is structured to minimize risk — $21M in real guarantees and heavy per-game incentives. The IOL is partially addressed: LG has a high-upside answer, but the Bradbury trade means the C position is now held by second-year player Jared Wilson with no proven veteran backup. IOL remains a moderate need, especially for draft-day depth.

contract.notes: Per Spotrac/OTC/Aaron Wilson: 3yr/$36.5M, $23.1M guaranteed, $11M signing bonus. Salaries in 2026 ($2.3M, gtd) and 2027 ($9.8M, gtd) are both guaranteed. Per-game bonuses of $60K/game + $180K workout bonus annually.

signings.analysis: Jones fills the EDGE2 role but is not a true pass-rush star. His 12.67M AAV ranks 34th among edge rushers — mid-to-low-tier starter money. He posted a career-high 7 sacks and 51 pressures split between Tennessee and Baltimore in 2025 and was the top pressure-rate player on the Ravens stretch (11.4%). Salaries in Years 1 and 2 are fully guaranteed, meaning he's a 2-year investment at minimum. With K'Lavon Chaisson (team sack leader, 10.5 sacks) and Anfernee Jennings both gone, Jones partially addresses the pass-rush void but does not replace Chaisson's production on his own. Harold Landry (knee concerns) and developing players (Ponder, Swinson) fill out the depth. EDGE remains a tier-1/2 need for the draft — the team is clearly still in the market.

contract.notes: Per NFL Network/Tom Pelissero: 1yr/$9M. Guarantees not publicly disclosed. One-year bridge deal.

signings.analysis: Byard is a 3x All-Pro coming off one of the best seasons of his career — led the NFL with 7 INTs in 2025 and earned first-team All-Pro honors. He reunites with Mike Vrabel (former Titans HC) and slots in as the starting S next to second-year Craig Woodson. At $9M AAV he's mid-tier for the position. However, it's a one-year deal for a 33-year-old, signaling a bridge arrangement rather than a long-term fix. The S need is PARTIALLY addressed: the 2026 starting spot is solved with a veteran of real quality, but the one-year structure means S will resurface as a need (likely in the draft or future FA) by 2027. Team still likely interested in a developmental S.

contract.notes: Per NFL Network/Mike Garafolo: 3yr/up to $12M, $6M fully guaranteed. Will earn $4.4M in 2026. Fullback designation.

signings.analysis: Gilliam is a fullback — a new position addition rather than a need-filler at RB. He played 80% of special teams snaps for the Bills over five seasons and 21% of offensive snaps. Josh McDaniels has historically used a fullback in his schemes and the Patriots lacked a legitimate FB in 2025. Three-year deal signals long-term commitment to the position. Not a traditional RB need-filler. Tagged under RB but is a specialized positional addition that also enhances ST. Minimal draft need impact at the FB/RB position.

contract.notes: Per ESPN/NFL Network: 3-year deal. Specific financial terms not publicly disclosed as of 2026-03-13. Classified as a lower-tier starter/TE2 deal given career undrafted status and role.

signings.analysis: Hill replaces Austin Hooper as the TE2 behind Hunter Henry. He had 22 starts over the last two years in Miami with 33 career catches and was valued primarily as a blocker and run-game asset, playing 55% of offensive snaps for the Dolphins. The three-year structure is a mild surprise given his modest receiving numbers (0 career TDs). He partially fills the TE depth hole but is a blocker-first backup, not a receiving upgrade. TE need is minimally addressed — Hunter Henry remains the clear starter. If the team wants a pass-catching TE2, it's still a viable draft target.

contract.notes: Per NFL Network/Ian Rapoport: 1-year deal. Financial terms not publicly disclosed. Minimum or near-minimum deal expected based on role.

signings.analysis: Brown reunites with Mike Vrabel (coached him with the Titans in 2023) and safeties coach Scott Booker. He's a special teams first player who played just 9 games in 2025 and had 37% of defensive snaps in 2024. Projects as S3 behind Byard and Craig Woodson. One-year, minimal-cost depth. Does not change the S need picture substantively.

contract.notes: Per Adam Schefter: 1-year deal. Financial terms not publicly disclosed. Depth/minimum range contract expected.

signings.analysis: Britt played 334 special teams snaps and just 148 defensive snaps for the Dolphins in 2025 — primarily a core ST player. He did start 11 games for Tampa Bay at ILB in 2024. He slots in as LB3/4 behind Robert Spillane and Christian Elliss and replaces some of the ST production lost with Jennings' and Tavai's departures. One-year deal means he's a stopgap/depth piece. LB remains a moderate need given departures.

contract.notes: Per Aaron Wilson/KPRC: 1yr/$1.145M veteran minimum deal. Signed March 5, pre-tampering period.

signings.analysis: Veteran minimum signing. Luketa appeared on the Rams' practice squad in 2025 and brings 3 career sacks. He's primarily a special teams contributor who appeared on 74% of ST snaps in his final two Cardinals seasons. Depth/camp body at EDGE. Does not address the significant pass-rush void left by Chaisson/Jennings departures. EDGE remains a critical need.

contract.notes: Per ESPN/Schefter: 2yr max $7.4M (base $4.4M + ~$3M incentives), $2M guaranteed. RFA re-signing.

signings.analysis: Re-signing the QB3 on a minimum-tier deal. DeVito dressed every game in 2025 but never appeared in a game. Drake Maye and Josh Dobbs are atop the depth chart. This is a continuity/roster management move. No impact on any positional need.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Doubs signing (4yr/$68M base, max $80M) is a genuine multi-year starter investment. He directly replaces Diggs' role and brings volume production. However, he has never topped 750 receiving yards in a season and was never WR1 in Green Bay. The team is still actively pursuing A.J. Brown via trade and WR remains a position they should address in the first two rounds of the draft. Drop from critical tier-1 to elevated tier-2 — still a premium need.

Vera-Tucker (3yr/$42M) fills the LG spot vacated when Jared Wilson shifts to C post-Bradbury trade. When healthy, Vera-Tucker is legitimately elite (PFF top-10 guard in 2024). BUT: he's missed 3 of 5 seasons to injury, sat out all of 2025, and hasn't played a game since 2024. The center shift (Wilson: rookie-year starter moving to new position) plus Vera-Tucker's injury risk keeps IOL firmly at tier-2. A reliable, proven center would be valuable in the draft (rounds 2-4).

Kevin Byard (1yr/$9M) fills the Hawkins void with an All-Pro-caliber starter. 2025 NFL interceptions leader. The pairing of Byard + Craig Woodson is strong for 2026. However, the 1-year deal on a 33-year-old means S resurfaces as a need in 2027. Reduce to tier-3 for 2026 draft — developmental S in rounds 4-6 is reasonable, but not a priority pick.

The net effect of free agency ESCALATES this need. Chaisson (10.5 sacks, team leader) and Jennings (run defense stalwart) both departed. Dre'Mont Jones (3yr/$36.5M, $12.67M AAV, 34th highest-paid edge) partially offsets but is a mid-tier addition, not a top-end replacement. Harold Landry has knee concerns. Elijah Ponder and Bradyn Swinson are developmental. The Patriots are still actively looking for more EDGE help per multiple beat reporters. EDGE should be the top priority in the 2026 draft — rounds 1-2 target.

Tonga's departure (3yr/$21M to Chiefs) removes the team's best run-stuffing DT and an emerging interior pass rusher. No free agent was signed to replace him. Cory Durden and Christian Barmore (charges dropped) are the primary interior options. IDL rises to a tier-2 need — team should target a run-stuffing DT in rounds 3-5 of the draft.

Vederian Lowe (swing tackle) departed to San Francisco on 2yr/$12M. No replacement OT was signed. Will Campbell (LT, 2nd year) is the starter and Morgan Moses (RT) returns, but there is now no reliable swing tackle behind them. OT depth is a real concern — rounds 3-5 draft target or potential late-FA addition.

Julian Hill (3yr deal) replaces Hooper as TE2 in a like-for-like blocker/depth swap. Hunter Henry remains the clear starter. The signing keeps TE stable as a tier-3 need. If the team wants a pass-catching TE2, rounds 3-5 of the draft remain viable, but this is not an urgent need.

Tavai released, Jennings released. Britt (1yr depth) and Luketa (minimum) added on ST-focused deals. Robert Spillane and Christian Elliss anchor the group with Jack Gibbens' status uncertain. The depth is thin but the starters are adequate. LB stays tier-3 — rounds 4-6 draft addition reasonable.

key_additions.headline: Patriots' new WR1 on a long-term deal replaces Diggs; 55 catches, 724 yards, 6 TDs in 2025
Former 14th-overall pick and PFF's top-10 guard (2024) fills LG; significant injury risk after missing all of 2025
Veteran pass rusher with career-high 7 sacks in 2025 partially fills Chaisson void; 2yr effective guarantee
3x All-Pro led NFL with 7 INTs in 2025; reunites with Vrabel in Foxborough to anchor safety position
Physical fullback on a 3-year deal signals long-term offensive identity shift under Josh McDaniels
Blocking TE2 steps in to replace Austin Hooper behind Hunter Henry on a 3-year deal
Vrabel reunion with depth safety adds secondary and special teams insurance
Core ST linebacker replaces Tavai/Jennings special teams production on 1-year depth deal
Veteran minimum EDGE depth piece and special teams contributor, camp competition role
QB3 retained on minimum deal; provides continuity behind Maye and Dobbs

key_losses.headline: Led team with 85 catches and 1,013 yards in 2025 Super Bowl run; released after one season, creating WR1 void
Team's top pass rusher (10.5 sacks, 73 pressures in 2025) departs, leaving critical EDGE hole
Starting center traded after 21-game season; Jared Wilson shifts to C as unproven second-year starter
Premier run-stopping DT and interior pass rusher departs, weakening IDL without a direct replacement
Starting safety departs for Baltimore, triggering Kevin Byard signing
Primary swing tackle departs, leaving team without a proven OT backup behind Campbell and Moses
Veteran TE2 returns to Atlanta; replaced by Julian Hill on a new 3-year deal
Team's longest-tenured player and stout run-defense edge setter released, compounding EDGE room losses
